title: 命案目睹记
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\136-命案目睹记-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Emma Crickenshaw meets with Claddock to discuss a letter she received from Matilda, who may be the widow of Emma's favorite brother, Edmund. Emma recalls receiving a telegram stating Matilda was returning to France around Christmas. While Emma believes the letter is genuine and hopes Edmund left a son, her father and brothers suspect it is a premeditated attempt at extortion. Emma's brother-in-law, Brian, believes the letter is real, but her brother Harold suggests a fraudster could have written it. Claddock notes the suspicious timing: Matilda's sudden return to Paris and subsequent silence after Emma replied. He also considers the legal implications regarding Edmund's potential heir and the value of Rutherford Manor.

Claddock concludes that the woman in the coffin and the woman who wrote the letter are likely unrelated. He instructs Detective Inspector Weatherall to travel to 126 Rue de Crésange in Paris to investigate if a woman claiming to be Mrs. Crickenshaw received mail there in late December. Later, Claddock receives a telegram from the Paris Police Headquarters stating that the provided information corresponds to Anna Stravinsky of the Mariinsky Ballet. Believing the Martina Crickenshaw case is nearing resolution, Claddock decides to take a ferry to Paris.

Meanwhile, at the Crickenshaw residence, Emma invites Miss Maple and her niece, Lucie Ainsworth, for tea. Harold and Cecil express skepticism toward Lucie, with Cecil questioning her trustworthiness due to her cleverness. Harold criticizes Emma for suggesting the corpse might be Edmund's French girlfriend, arguing that such claims make the family appear suspicious to the police.
